description_long: This kiosk is a small, rectangular brick structure with a prominent hipped roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The roof extends outward, forming a wide overhang supported by two wooden posts at the front, providing shelter for the entrance area. The walls are constructed from red and brown bricks, and the base is set on a patterned stone or tile platform. The front features a single wooden door and a large rectangular window opening, while the side and rear walls are mostly solid brick with minimal openings. The overall design is utilitarian, with a rustic touch from the exposed brickwork and wooden elements. The kiosk appears sturdy and functional, suitable for use as a small retail or service point in an urban environment.
description: A small brick kiosk with a hipped tile roof, wide overhang, and a front entrance with a door and window. The structure is set on a tiled platform and features rustic, utilitarian design elements.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the hipped roof, brick wall, and the wide roof overhang with one supporting post visible.
description_view_1: The rear view displays the full expanse of the tiled roof, a solid brick wall, and a single wooden door on the right side.
description_view_2: The right side view highlights the roof overhang, brick wall, and a partial view of the kiosk's rear and front features.
description_view_3: The front view reveals the main entrance with a wooden door, a large rectangular window opening, and two supporting posts under the roof overhang.
attribute_window_presence: one rectangular window at the front
attribute_door_configuration: one single wooden door at the front right
attribute_roof_design: hipped roof with reddish-brown tiles, wide overhang on all sides, supported by wooden posts at the front
attribute_architectural_style: rustic utilitarian with traditional brickwork and simple wooden elements
